title: Inventory Aging — Atlanta Entry Level
executive_summary: Atlanta entry level inventory is aging materially, with 5.8% of active listings (14 homes) exceeding 90 days on market and average DOM now at 52.9 days. The trend has been worsening for multiple consecutive weeks, indicating a structural demand-supply imbalance rather than a temporary seasonal blip. Without intervention, these homes will require markdown pricing to clear, compressing resale margins from current 6.2%.
recommended_actions: Market-ops team: Implement an immediate 3% price reduction on all Atlanta entry level listings exceeding 75 days DOM. For homes over 100 days, apply a 5% reduction. Execute price changes by end of business Friday.

Pricing team: Review acquisition offers in Atlanta entry level for the past 8 weeks and identify if any AVM segments or zip codes show systematic overvaluation at resale. Pause new acquisitions in zip codes with 3+ aged homes until the resale velocity audit is complete.

Analytics team: Add a leading indicator dashboard tracking Atlanta buyer demand signals (showings per listing, offer receipt rate) to provide earlier warning of demand softening. Target: 2-week lead time before DOM breach. Present dashboard to market-ops lead by next Monday.

title: Funnel Deterioration — Dallas Premium
executive_summary: Dallas premium is showing a deteriorating seller conversion funnel: lead-to-offer rate has fallen to 36.7% (down ~3% WoW for multiple consecutive weeks), reducing overall funnel efficiency to 10.0%. At 60 leads this week, the degraded conversion rate translates to approximately 12 fewer offers generated versus the historical baseline — a volume shortfall that will flow through to close counts in 2-3 weeks if not addressed.
recommended_actions: Growth team: Pull the Dallas offer funnel drop-off report by step (lead received → offer generated → offer viewed → offer accepted) for the past 3 weeks and identify the specific step where fall-off increased. Report findings to leadership by Wednesday.

Operations team: Audit Dallas offer turnaround SLA compliance for the past 2 weeks. If median offer generation time exceeds 24 hours, escalate to ops capacity planning and consider temporary underwriting surge support. SLA audit to be completed by EOD tomorrow.

Pricing team: Compare Dallas premium Opendoor offers against Zillow/Offerpad comparable offers for the same homes (use the 10 most recent rejections). If Opendoor is systematically below competitor offers by >2%, initiate a competitive adjustment review this week.

title: Pricing Misalignment — Phoenix Mid Tier
executive_summary: Opendoor's offer prices in Phoenix mid tier are running 5.8% above market estimated values, triggering a seller acceptance rate collapse to 49.4% — down roughly 12 percentage points from prior-week levels. At current offer volume, this misalignment is costing approximately 9 accepted offers per week and eroding acquisition margins to 1.4%.
recommended_actions: Pricing team: Pull all Phoenix mid tier comparable sales from the past 30 days and audit the AVM inputs by zip code. Apply a manual -3% offer adjustment across the segment by EOD tomorrow while the model recalibration is completed.

Analytics team: Set a real-time alert threshold so that any market/segment combination where avg_offer_vs_market_pct exceeds +2% for 3 consecutive days triggers an automated Slack notification to the Pricing Lead. Implement by end of this sprint.

Operations team: Place a temporary hold on Phoenix mid tier acquisitions above $450K list price until the AVM recalibration is validated — this caps the margin exposure while preserving volume on the lower end of the segment. Review hold in 72 hours.
